string.text: Master on/off switch for the floating quest marker cards.
Show a card over the NPC the game is currently pointing you at for a task: the "go talk to X" step and the "return to the giver" turn-ins.

Show a card over the stalker who is currently looking for a guide to escort them (the "Need a guide?" job). Detected via the guide job's own dialogue condition, so it matches exactly when that action is available, and only one stalker offers it at a time. Requires the guide-job system (ships with GAMMA); without it this has no effect.

Show a card ("LOOKING FOR WORK") over nearby friendly stalkers you could recruit as a companion right now: friendly, part of a squad, and within your companion limit. Detected without a full scan. A party-full check skips it entirely when you can't recruit anyone, then only on-screen stalkers within the appear distance are tested, on a slow 3-second cadence. Uses the game's own recruit-dialog eligibility check. Requires the companion system (ships with GAMMA); without it this has no effect.

Show a card ("GUIDE" plus the guide glyph) over NPCs offering fast-travel guide services, the ones your PDA marks with the Guide icon. The Service card style option below can switch it to an icon-only chip or plain text. Detected from the engine's own PDA map-spot registry, so it always matches the map legend.

Show a card over traders, technicians, barkeeps and medics, each with its header ("TRADER" / "TECHNICIAN" / "BARKEEP" / "MEDIC") and role glyph. The Service card style option below can switch them to icon-only chips or plain text. Detected from the engine's PDA map-spot registry; the exact service is read from the NPC's actual trade file, so a technician labelled as a trader by the level still shows the technician glyph.

Show a card ("VIP" plus a star glyph) over story and faction important characters, the ones your PDA marks with the Important Character or quest-NPC icon. The Service card style option below can switch it to an icon-only chip or plain text. Off by default, since busy hubs already show plenty of cards.

Tint each card (the leader line, edge bar, header text and head node) with the NPC's faction colour instead of the default gold accent. Colours match the faction palette used by the GAMMA DB site. NPCs of a factionless community (e.g. zombies, monsters) keep the gold accent.

Show the NPC's faction emblem on the state cards (quest turn-in, "needs a guide", recruitable companion), inlined before the text. Service and important-character cards keep their role glyph (trader, medic, guide, etc.) instead. The emblems are the game's own PDA faction patches (from G.A.M.M.A. UI, so they match the faction-relations screen and any HD patch mod you have installed). NPCs of a factionless community show no emblem.

Shorten a long military rank at the start of an NPC's name so it fits the card: "Senior Sergeant Abdurakhmanov" becomes "Snr Sgt Abdurakhmanov". Only the leading rank is abbreviated; the surname is left alone. Mostly affects Duty and Military stalkers.

How the service/important cards (trader, technician, barkeep, medic, guide, important character) are drawn. "Icon chip": a compact plate with just the role glyph. "Text + icon": the full card with header, name and the glyph. "Text only": the full card with no glyph. The glyphs are bundled with the mod, so this works on its own.
Draw the small dot at the NPC's head where the leader line begins. Turn off for just the line and card.
Gently breathe the soft glow halo around the head node dot (a slow, subtle pulse) to draw the eye to the objective. The dot itself stays steady. Turn off for a completely static glow.
Play a subtle PDA alert beep the first time each NPC's card comes into view. A per-NPC cooldown stops brief glances or the NPC ducking behind cover from re-triggering it.
Loudness of the PDA sighting chirp (0 = silent, 1 = full). Only applies when "PDA chirp on sighting" is on.
How far away the card starts to fade in as you approach. Beyond this it is hidden.
Within this range the card is fully solid. Between this and the appear distance it fades. Keep it below the appear distance.

Hide the card when the NPC is behind a wall or other cover, instead of showing through it. Requires the shared demonized_geometry_ray utility (bundled with many GAMMA mods, including both Catspaw addons); without it this setting has no effect.
How often each NPC's line of sight is re-tested. Lower reacts faster to going behind cover but costs more performance; higher is cheaper but slower to hide. Default 150.

How long the card keeps showing after the NPC slips behind cover, to stop flicker from brief or partial occlusions (a doorway, a passing object). Higher is steadier but lingers longer through cover; 0 hides the instant line of sight is lost. Default 250.
Nudge the node up (+) or down (-) from the NPC's left-shoulder bone. The default (0) sits right on the shoulder near the neck; negative drops it toward the mid-left torso.
Manual sideways nudge along the NPC's own body axis, toward their right (+) or left (-). Tracks their facing as they turn. Added on top of the auto shoulder placement below.

Automatically place the node on whichever shoulder sits on the card's side of the screen, so the leader line meets the near shoulder instead of crossing the NPC's body. Both shoulders are tested on-screen and the one toward the card is picked, so it works at any camera angle and NPC facing. Turn off to place the node purely with the manual offset above.
How far onto the shoulder the auto placement pushes the node (roughly a torso half-width). Only used when Auto shoulder side is on.
Horizontal distance from the head to the card. The card flips to the other side automatically near the screen edge.
How far above the head the card floats.
Thickness of the diagonal line connecting the head to the card.
Opacity of the leader line at full visibility (0 = invisible, 255 = solid).
Opacity of the dark card plate at full visibility (0 = invisible, 255 = solid).
Full size of the round dot at the NPC's head, reached when you are within the full-opacity distance. Ignored when Show head node dot is off.
How small the dot shrinks at the appear distance, as a fraction of its full size. It grows smoothly to full size as you approach. 1.0 = no distance scaling.
How quickly the card eases toward the NPC's head. 1 = instant (snaps / jitters), lower = smoother but laggier. Framerate-independent. Default 0.35.
Position jumps larger than this teleport instead of gliding, so the card doesn't slide across the screen when the NPC's head first appears or moves suddenly.
Master alpha of the accent (line / edge bar / header / node).
Red component of the accent colour (0-255).
Green component of the accent colour (0-255).
Blue component of the accent colour (0-255). Defaults give a soft desaturated gold (224, 196, 122).
Write IQM diagnostic lines (line-of-sight checks, card-slot overflow, sound playback, per-slot coords) to the console/log. For troubleshooting; off by default.
